provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Rural Energy Regulations, 2011.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations: “ Act ” means the Rural Energy Act; [ Act No 8 of 2005 ] “ Agency ” means the Rural Energy Agency established under section 14 of the Act ; “ Authority ” means the Energy and Water Utilities Regulatory Authority (EWURA) established under the Energy and Water Utilities Regulatory Act ; [Cap. 414] “ grid based commercial generation ” means units which are generated from the grid connected sources and commercially sold to end users; “ generation in specified isolate systems ” means units which are generated from the isolated sources and commercially sold to end users or private consumption; “ generation for private use ” means units which are metered at the point of dispatch to the consumer or own consumption fora generator with an installed capacity of above 1 Megawatt (MW); “ grid price ” means the price of one unit of electricity at the entry point to the national grid; “ gross revenue ” for the purpose of— (a) commercial generation, means a revenue derived from electricity generated from the grid connected sources and commercially sold to end users; and (b) private consumption generation, means an equivalent of electricity revenue if the same electricity is bought from the grid at the applicable tariff; “ Levy Collector ” means a generator or an agent responsible for collection of levy payable to the Agency ; “ Trust Agent ” means the agent appointed under the provision of section 23 of the Act ; “ Project developer ” means a developer of, or an investor in, or an operator of activities and investments associated with the provision of modern energy services in a locality in rural area of Mainland Tanzania; “ grant ” means an award of money by the Fund to a qualified developer for the purpose of developing and implementing a project ; “ qualified developer ” means a developer that satisfies the criteria for qualification as established by the Board; “ Board "means the Board of Directors established under section 6 of the Act ; “ modern energy ” means energy that is based on petroleum, electricity or any other energy form that has commercialized market channels, a higher heating or energy heating content value than traditional biomass fuel, which may be easily transported, stored and utilized; “ Rural Energy Fund ” means Rural Energy Fund established under section 17 of the Act , and includes financial resources set aside for rural energy development; “ rural area ” means an area outside an urban authority; and “ project ” means the activities and investment associated with the provision of modern energy services in a locality of a rural area in Mainland Tanzania. [Cap. 321] Part II – Electricity levy

provisions.title: Charging of levy
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of the Act , every electricity generator shall be liable to pay electricity levy to the Agency upon commencement of electricity generation. (2), Electricity levy shall be charged and payable to the Rural Energy Fund on monthly basis calculated at a rate of up to 5% of sales revenue in respect of— (a) revenue collected from electricity generated from the grid connected sources and commercially sold to end users; or (b) revenue collected from electricity generators in isolated systems; (c) an equivalent value of electricity generated for private consumption as if the same electricity is bought from the grid at the applicable tariff.

provisions.title: Payment of levy
provisions.content: (1) The levy for the month shall be payable to the fund on or before: (a) for prepaid customers, 30 th day of the following month; (b) for the post-paid customers, 30 th day of the second month subsequent to the month of sale. (2) The Levy Collector shall prepare and submit a schedule combining prepaid and post paid customers on 15 th day of the payment month showing sales revenue and computation of the levy. (3) The Agency shall serve the Levy Collector with the demand note basing on the schedule of sales revenue and computed levy immediately after receiving the schedule.

provisions.title: Reminder notice
provisions.content: The Agency shall issue a reminder notice to the electricity generator to pay the levy in accordance with the provisions of these Regulations.

provisions.title: Penalty for failure to pay the prescribed levy
provisions.content: (1) The electricity generator who fails to pay the prescribed levy on the due date as provided under these Regulations shall be liable to pay a sum equal to five percentum of the amount unpaid as penalty for each month or part of a month following the date when the payment should have been made. (2) The Agency shall, in consultation with the Authority , initiate a process to suspend generation licence of the generator who fails to pay the levy for three consecutive months. Part III – Administration of grant payments

provisions.title: Processing of fund application
provisions.content: (1) The Agency shall, by way of advertisement or any other way as the Agency deems fit, issue a notice inviting developers to submit applications for grant support for projects. (2) The applications shall be submitted in the manner prescribed in the model document for applications and approval for grants from the Fund issued by the Agency .

provisions.title: Appointment of a Trust Agent
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall appoint a Trust Agent for the administration of grant payments under the Act . (2) The Trust Agent shall be responsible for the administration of grant payments, including financial disbursement, verification and monitoring activities.

provisions.title: Functions of the Trust Agent
provisions.content: (1) In the course of financial disbursement, the Trust Agent shall verify all disbursement requests for correctness; (a) ensure that all payment documentation is in order and in accordance with any special requirements as determined by the Board in the contract; (b) ensure that the funds are disbursed to the project developer in accordance with the Project Implementation Schedule provided for in the contract, but not later than 15 days after being instructed by the Agency ; (c) maintain and update accounting records relating to disbursements; and (d) prepare appropriate statements of expenditure, in accordance with the financial management guidelines. (2) In the course of monitoring and verification, the Trust Agent shall; (a) submit quarterly reports to the Agency detailing the following information: (i) correlation between disbursed funds and physical project implementation; (ii) potential project risks that may cause delays or may halt the project ; and (iii) any other information that may be deemed important for smooth implementation of the project . (b) conduct field visits to ensure satisfactory or smooth implementation of the project ; (c) conduct regular meetings with the project developer at least once per quarter to discuss issues notice during site visits; (d) give adequate prior notice of meetings to the project developer specifying information or reports that may be required; and (e) prepare and submit to the Agency annual financial statement in a satisfactory and timely manner. (3) The reports referred to in sub-regulation (2)(a) shall be submitted timely; (a) in electronic and hard copy; and (b) in accordance with the reporting format established by the Agency . (4) All reports prepared by the Trust Agent shall be the property of the Agency . (5) The Trust Agent shall not be allowed to use any information contained in the report for any other purpose unless for purposes of the Act , these Regulations and by permission of the Agency . Part IV – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Supporting Energy End Use Infrastructure Scheme
provisions.content: The Agency shall ensure that energy is used productively for economic gain by promoting energy demand in the rural areas through supporting development of Energy End Use Infrastructure Scheme (EEIS) such as irrigation schemes, agro processing, mining and industries.

provisions.title: Powers to review, amend or suspend the EEIS
provisions.content: The Rural Energy Board shall have a right to review, amend or suspend the EEIS scheme.

provisions.title: Persons eligible to benefit from EEIS
provisions.content: The persons eligible to benefit from the EEIS shall be Tanzanian citizens, permanent residents and properly registered organizations, provided that an applicant shall be required to prove capacity to embark on a new EEIS projects or to expand or rehabilitate an existing EEIS project and the ability to run the project in a viable and sustainable manner.

provisions.title: Priority for use of funds
provisions.content: During the implementation of EEIS, the Agency shall give priority to community based establishments such as schools, health centers and registered organizations.
